message:
feat: add list_sessions() to Provider trait for accurate multi-session enumeration

DB-backed and multi-session providers (OpenCode, Cursor, Aider) store multiple
sessions in a single file/database. The existing `casr list` used directory
walking which only discovered one session per file. This adds an opt-in
`list_sessions()` method to the Provider trait that these providers override
to enumerate all sessions from their storage.

- Add `list_sessions()` to Provider trait with default None (bd-15t)
- Implement for OpenCode: queries all session IDs from SQLite (bd-15t)
- Implement for Cursor: enumerates all composer IDs from cursorDiskKV (bd-q0j)
- Implement for Aider: splits history files on session headers (bd-2m9)
- Modify cmd_list to prefer list_sessions() over directory walking
- Add 33 new OpenCode inline tests (7 → 38) covering parse_parts edge cases,
  build_parts, role_to_opencode, workspace_from_db_path, dedup_existing_files,
  writer/reader edge cases, and list_sessions (bd-e65)
- Close bd-16s epic (all 12 subtasks completed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fixed AmbiguousSessionId with file-existence checks and --force flags. Fixed PiAgent alias (pi not pia). All quality gates green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2d7","title":"Add cross-provider test suite for all 14 providers (roundtrip, scalability, golden output)","description":"## Overview

After all 14 providers are implemented and wired, add comprehensive cross-provider tests that validate the full pipeline (read → canonical → write) works correctly for every provider combination.

This bead covers test infrastructure that spans multiple providers, NOT the per-provider unit tests (which are part of each provider's individual bead).

## Test Categories

### 1. Roundtrip Tests (`tests/roundtrip_test.rs`)
- For each provider: create synthetic native session → read → canonical → write → read again → compare
- Verify message count, roles, content, timestamps survive the roundtrip
- Test all 14 providers (currently tests exist for ~9)

### 2. Cross-Provider Pipeline Tests
- For each (source, target) pair where source != target:
  - Read source session → canonical → write to target → verify target file is valid
- Focus on the most common pairs: claude-code↔codex, gemini↔claude-code, etc.
- At minimum, test every new provider as both source and target against claude-code

### 3. Golden Output Tests (`tests/golden_output_test.rs`)
- For each new provider, add a golden input file under `tests/fixtures/` and a golden canonical output
- Verify that `read_session(golden_input)` produces exactly the golden canonical output
- This catches regressions in the reader logic

### 4. Scalability Tests (`tests/scalability_test.rs`)
- Verify that reading/writing large sessions (1000+ messages) works without excessive memory or time
- Test with at least one JSONL-based and one JSON-based provider

### 5. Discovery Tests (`tests/discovery_test.rs`)
- Test that `infer_provider_for_path()` correctly identifies all 14 providers
- Test ambiguous cases (e.g., JSONL files that could be multiple providers)
- Test the full `resolve_session()` flow for each new provider

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Roundtrip tests cover all 14 providers
- [ ] At least 5 cross-provider pipeline tests for new providers
- [ ] Golden output tests for all 5 new providers
- [ ] All tests pass, no regressions
- [ ] Total test count increases by 30+ from these new tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:09:54.326659226Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:32:46.346622386Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T03:32:46.346602969Z","close_reason":"Roundtrip tests added for all 6 new providers (10 tests), total 33 roundtrip tests passing across all 14 providers","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2d7","depends_on_id":"bd-352","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:11:35.163087383Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Added test. 431 lib tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2rl","title":"EPIC: Canonical Session Model","description":"Define the canonical intermediate representation (IR) for coding agent sessions. This is the Rosetta Stone of casr: every provider's native format is normalized into this IR, and every target format is generated from it.

Port/adapt the conversion-relevant data types and helper logic from CASS (coding_agent_session_search), but keep the IR focused on *session conversion*, not CASS's indexing/search features.

The canonical model must capture:
- ordered messages with roles (user/assistant/tool/system)
- timestamps (normalized to epoch millis)
- workspace/cwd when available
- session identity (source id and generated target id)
- model name (session-level convenience + message-level author)
- tool calls/results when representable
- provider-specific passthrough fields (lossless preservation via `session.metadata` and `message.extra`)

Intentional non-goals (do NOT pull in CASS-only indexing baggage):
- snippet extraction/indexing types (casr can derive summaries from message content without storing snippets as first-class IR fields)
- database ids / remote-origin fields

## Success Criteria

- `CanonicalSession`/`CanonicalMessage` are expressive enough to write plausible sessions for core providers.
- Provider-specific fields that cannot be canonically represented are preserved in `metadata`/`extra`.
- Timestamp and content normalization helpers are centralized and exhaustively unit-tested.
- IR choices are documented so writer implementations have clear mapping rules.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:36:44.447987709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:41:43.484209368Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:41:43.484180534Z","close_reason":"All 3 children completed: CanonicalSession/CanonicalMessage types defined (bd-2rl.1), Provider trait defined (bd-2rl.2), all 5 content normalization helpers implemented (bd-2rl.3). The canonical IR is complete and ready for readers/writers to use.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rl","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:04.457331048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2rl.1","title":"Define CanonicalSession and CanonicalMessage types","description":"Port and adapt the core session data model from CASS (conversion-relevant subset). Define these types in `src/model.rs`.

## Types

`CanonicalSession`:
- `session_id: String`
- `provider_slug: String` (source provider slug: `claude-code`, `codex`, `gemini`)
- `workspace: Option<PathBuf>` (project root / cwd)
- `title: Option<String>`
- `started_at: Option<i64>` (epoch millis)
- `ended_at: Option<i64>` (epoch millis)
- `messages: Vec<CanonicalMessage>`
- `metadata: serde_json::Value` (provider-specific extras that don't map to canonical fields; default `{}`)
- `source_path: Option<PathBuf>` (native file location when known)
- `model_name: Option<String>` (session-level convenience; may be derived from per-message author/model)

`CanonicalMessage`:
- `idx: usize` (0-based sequential order)
- `role: MessageRole`
- `content: String` (primary human-readable text)
- `timestamp: Option<i64>` (epoch millis)
- `author: Option<String>` (model name or identifier)
- `tool_calls: Vec<ToolCall>`
- `tool_results: Vec<ToolResult>`
- `extra: serde_json::Value` (provider-specific raw fields; default `{}`; SHOULD include raw native record/content blocks when available)

`MessageRole` enum:
- `User`
- `Assistant`
- `Tool`
- `System`
- `Other(String)`

Naming note:
- CASS uses "Agent" in some places; casr uses `Assistant` as the canonical variant. Normalization helpers should map `agent`/`model` -> `Assistant`.

Tool structs:
- `ToolCall { id: Option<String>, name: String, arguments: serde_json::Value }`
- `ToolResult { call_id: Option<String>, content: String, is_error: bool }`

Derives:
- All types derive `Debug`, `Clone`, `Serialize`, `Deserialize`.
- `CanonicalSession` and `CanonicalMessage` derive `PartialEq` for testing.

## Intentional Omissions vs CASS

Do NOT pull in CASS indexing/search-only fields (e.g., Snippet extraction/index ids). casr only needs conversion fidelity; summaries can be derived from message content.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Types compile cleanly with serde derives and `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.
- Defaults for `metadata`/`extra` are stable (empty object, not null) to simplify writers.
- Canonical model supports representing tool calls/results without forcing provider-specific schemas.
- Unit tests validate serde round-trip and equality semantics for representative sessions/messages.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:39:20.241612244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:39:33.728375004Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:39:33.728355216Z","close_reason":"All types defined in src/model.rs: CanonicalSession (10 fields), CanonicalMessage (8 fields), MessageRole (5 variants), ToolCall, ToolResult. All derive Debug+Clone+Serialize+Deserialize, Session/Message also PartialEq. CASS naming note (Agent→Assistant) and deliberately omitted fields documented in module doc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rl.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:10.438561462Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2rl.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:24.727876844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2rl.2","title":"Define Provider trait with read/write/detect methods","description":"Define the Provider trait in `src/providers/mod.rs`. This is the core abstraction that each provider (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, and future providers) implements.

## Trait Surface

```rust
pub trait Provider {
    /// Human-readable name (e.g. "Claude Code")
    fn name(&self) -> &str;

    /// Stable slug used in logs/metadata (e.g. "claude-code", "codex", "gemini")
    fn slug(&self) -> &str;

    /// CLI alias used in `casr <alias> resume ...` (e.g. "cc", "cod", "gmi")
    fn cli_alias(&self) -> &str;

    /// Detect presence of the provider binary + session roots.
    /// This MUST be evidence-rich (not a single bool).
    fn detect(&self) -> DetectionResult;

    /// Candidate session roots for this provider (respects *_HOME overrides).
    fn session_roots(&self) -> Vec<std::path::PathBuf>;

    /// Fast ownership check: return the native session file path if this provider owns `session_id`.
    /// Should avoid full file parsing when possible.
    fn owns_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> Option<std::path::PathBuf>;

    /// Read a session from native format into canonical IR.
    fn read_session(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> anyhow::Result<CanonicalSession>;

    /// Write a canonical session into this provider's native format.
    /// Returns all paths written + generated session_id.
    fn write_session(&self, session: &CanonicalSession, opts: &WriteOptions)
        -> anyhow::Result<WrittenSession>;

    /// The resume command string for this provider (e.g. `claude --resume <id>`).
    fn resume_command(&self, session_id: &str) -> String;
}
```

Also define:

```rust
pub struct DetectionResult {
    pub binary: Option<BinaryEvidence>,
    pub roots: Vec<RootEvidence>,
    pub notes: Vec<String>,
}

pub struct BinaryEvidence {
    pub exe: std::path::PathBuf,
    pub version: Option<String>,
}

pub struct RootEvidence {
    pub path: std::path::PathBuf,
    pub exists: bool,
    pub readable: bool,
    pub writable: bool,
    pub evidence: Vec<String>,
}

impl DetectionResult {
    pub fn can_read(&self) -> bool { /* any readable root */ }
    pub fn can_write(&self) -> bool { /* any writable root OR creatable root */ }
    pub fn can_resume(&self) -> bool { self.binary.is_some() }
}

pub struct WrittenSession {
    pub paths: Vec<std::path::PathBuf>,
    pub session_id: String,
    pub resume_command: String,
    pub backup_paths: Vec<std::path::PathBuf>,
}

pub struct WriteOptions {
    pub force: bool,
}
```

## Detection Semantics (Critical For UX)

- `detect()` MUST distinguish:
  - binary present vs missing (`can_resume()`),
  - session roots present/readable (`can_read()`),
  - session roots writable/creatable (`can_write()`).
- casr should be able to READ sessions even when the provider binary is not installed.
- casr should be able to WRITE sessions as long as roots are writable/creatable, even if the binary is missing (warn in UX).

## Conflict + Backup Policy

- Conflict/`--force` handling MUST live in exactly one place: `atomic_write` (bd-ikb.3).
- Provider writers call `atomic_write`; the pipeline should not duplicate pre-checks (avoid TOCTOU).
- `WrittenSession.backup_paths` is surfaced to UX so users can find restored/overwritten files.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `Provider` is object-safe and storable as `Box<dyn Provider>` in a registry.
- `DetectionResult` is rich enough to power `casr providers` output (binary evidence + root evidence + notes).
- Env var overrides are supported for root derivation: `CLAUDE_HOME`, `CODEX_HOME`, `GEMINI_HOME`.
- `write_session()` uses `WriteOptions.force` and surfaces backup paths when overwriting.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:39:32.295047628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:39:53.161795562Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:39:53.161775054Z","close_reason":"Provider trait already defined in src/providers/mod.rs: 9 methods (name, slug, cli_alias, detect, session_roots, owns_session, read_session, write_session, resume_command). Object-safe (Send+Sync). WriteOptions{force}, WrittenSession{paths,session_id,resume_command,backup_path}, DetectionResult in discovery.rs. Design note on conflict handling documented in trait comments.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rl.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:10.241171305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2rl.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:24.800231584Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
